ogTitle: Peakhurst Heights vs Pearl Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Peakhurst Heights and Pearl Beach.
heading: Peakhurst Heights vs Pearl Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Peakhurst Heights, New South Wales and Pearl Beach, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pearl Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Peakhurst Heights (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Peakhurst Heights or Pearl Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Peakhurst Heights has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Pearl Beach scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Peakhurst Heights scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Pearl Beach scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Peakhurst Heights or Pearl Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pearl Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
